When something ships, it moves *out* of here into the changelog; only live + in-flight + next-action state stays.

## What's Live in Prod

- **Evryn product = `v0.2.8`** (`evryn-backend` tag `v0.2.8` / `main` `690e0d0`), on Railway Oregon/us-west-2, deployed 2026-07-22. Clean boot; M1-protected + Healthchecks-monitored. **`v0.2.8` shipped ③ Runtime Bookkeeping (Step 57 — verdict-time user-creation, raw `supabase_upsert` removed, operator-only `correct_user_field`) + Step-78 Half A real `/health` + 54a/54c + the Anthropic key-split (ADR-053, dedicated Evryn-Production workspace/key) + dead-var cleanup. ZERO prod migration.** Full ledger: `docs/deploy-log.md`.
- **Two big cost levers stay DORMANT on `v0.2.8`** (Clustering, Step 58/ADR-049 · Haiku pre-screen, Step 44). Both env-gated; `config.ts` treats *unset* identically to `off`. On the running `v0.2.8` deployment `CLUSTERING_MODE` and `HAIKU_SCREEN_MODE` are both **UNSET** → each runs as the safe no-op `off` (③ did not touch the lever code). Flipping them on is a deliberate pre-Mark go-live action; zero prod behavior change until then.
- **`evryn-backend` `main` == the deployed `v0.2.8`** (`690e0d0`) — synced to origin; the only in-flight code delta anywhere is the WebFetch-timeout fast-follow (`dc/webfetch-timeout` worktree, unmerged — see below).
- **Team runtime (`evryn-team-runtime`) is deployed to Railway but PAUSED / boot-paused by construction** — dormant, wakes nobody. Autonomy stays OFF until the Meta-Meeting. Evryn prod is untouched by all team-runtime work.

## In Flight — Product Wave (critical path to Mark-live)

*(③ Runtime Bookkeeping / Step 57 SHIPPED `v0.2.8` 2026-07-22 → see What's Live. Remaining ③ #lock doc-flips owed — SPRINT-57→DONE, ARCHITECTURE ③-absorption, operator-guide, BUILD markers — pending Justin's SCM vet.)*

- **Critical path now: ③ #lock finish → Gate-B → flip Clustering + Haiku ON → gatekeeper.** **Gate-A** stays co-signed GO (13/13, Megan fictional gatekeeper on `main`); **Gate-B** (SPRINT Step 88B — mandatory pre-Mark full create-from-zero staging live-fire, everything active together) is the remaining gate, fires after Justin flips `SEND_ENABLED` — a FRESH dedicated reviewer owns the verdict, AC3 supports staging mechanics, AC0 gates.
- **WebFetch-timeout fast-follow (AC2)** — the ONE in-flight code delta: a wall-clock `EVRYN_QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS` AbortController on `runEvrynQuery` so a hung fetch can't wedge the poll loop (real Mark-live risk). One tip commit on `dc/webfetch-timeout` (`bf23bca`), **on a stale base (~23 behind `v0.2.8`) → needs a rebase onto `main` before QC/merge.** Still owes: AC2's live-path hang exercise (held Gate-A DEV state = the evidence; DO NOT WIPE) + the ADR + **Justin's design question** (per-turn inter-message-silence watchdog vs. the flat cap; NB-2 = `reflection.md`'s "never a hard cap").
- **Step 78 (liveness/health) — NOW FULLY LIVE.** Half A (real 503-when-wedged `/health`) shipped with `v0.2.8`; Half B (dashboard's 5 per-subsystem lights, Vercel) went live-inert 2026-07-20. ⭐ **POST-DEPLOY CHECK OWED: verify the Half-B dashboard health lights came alive** (they rendered idle "—" until Half A deployed — Half A is now live, so they should read real status).
- **SPRINT 91 — 🔴 MARK-BLOCKER (AC2, now that ③ merged).** Halt-resume skips gatekeeper lane-resolution → phantom lead + empty-profile triage (`resumeParkedItem` in `process.ts`; DIRECT-message half exposed regardless of clustering). Fix immediately after ③ (same `process.ts` file), before Mark forwards. · **SPRINT 92 (AC0, now unblocked)** — `schema-reference.md` full re-pull (3 live tables missing; interim DO-NOT-TRUST banner up) + close the protocol-gap (re-pull fires on memory, needs a structural migration-time trigger).
- **AC4 = Weekend architecture capture — the priority right after the deploy (Justin, 2026-07-22).** A fresh, fully-loaded dedicated AC (named AC4) re-derives the napkin from first principles: the backend growing into a god-module (`triage/classify.ts`, ~2,606 lines — core Evryn service fused into a feature file → punishing to load). Proposal shape: cheap regenerable dependency **map** + a Code Atlas soon; **extract `classify.ts`** in a quiet gap; build v0.3 subsystems with clean seams. Runs while AC2/AC3 do their next set. Napkin (explicitly *not* settled): `docs/working/2026.07.19-ac-justin-restructure-napkin.md`.

## Strategic Pivot: Evryn Product First

Team agent build was paused to focus on building the Evryn product MVP (v0.2 "Gatekeeper's Inbox") for Mark (real-Mark = Mark Titus, Seattle filmmaker / August Island Pictures / Eva's Wild) first. Real-Mark "we're ready" email SENT 2026-06-11; **Mark is not yet forwarding** (prod idle until he does). Team agent build has since resumed.

## How AC Works Now

AC drives DC (build) and QC (review) as **subagents** — protocol `docs/protocols/ac-orchestration-protocol.md`. QC verifies every real code change; AC promotes QC's standing patterns into her CLAUDE.md. Ping-by-default on `#team-alerts`. ARCH + BUILD docs: AC holds edit rights, editing needs Justin's explicit auth first. Lane-owning ACs own their lane's design; AC0 owns cross-lane orchestration + collisions + merge gates.

**Load-list home = the cascade** (as of 2026-07-22): for any build, its self-sufficient full-load list is `docs/protocols/load-cascades/<build>-full-cascade.md`; *this* file is the **freshness-authority** the cascade cross-checks (which build/sprint is active *now*). Changelogs are **per-repo** — each repo's own `CHANGELOG.md` plus the meta one (`_evryn-meta/CHANGELOG.md`), which every AC skims on load.
